title: mesosphaerum lacustre
intro: Mesosphaerum lacustre, also known as "water-horehound" or "marsh bugleweed," is a plant species that belongs to the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The health benefits of Mesosphaerum lacustre include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and ease symptoms of bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Mesosphaerum lacustre often involve infusions or decoctions made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical treatment.
